creator: fabian@wenks.ch
text:
Hello Mathieu

I have just updated my Ports [1] and got the update from 
dns/bind99 (bind99-9.9.4_2 -> bind99-9.9.4_9) on FreeBSD 
9.1-RELEASE-p9.
A few days ago with the update of an other port (in my case 
jabberd) openssl from the Ports was pulled in. Before I had only 
system openssl installed. After the upgrade with restart of 
bind99 with '/etc/rc.d/named restart' I got the same errors as 
you already reported yourself in this PR.

I did the following to fix this:
mkdir -p /var/named/usr/local/lib/engines/
cp -p /usr/local/lib/engines/* /var/named/usr/local/lib/engines/

As I see it, the fix in r338882 [2] did not touch / change 
anything related to lib/engines. I think somehow updating 
lib/engines in the bind / named chroot should be done from the 
openssl port, but sure it needs a restart of named to take 
effect. Eventually this is something which should go into the 
/etc/rc.d/named script to also update / create the lib/engines in 
the named chroot if openssl is installed from Ports.


   [1]
   root@superman:/usr/ports # svn info | grep 'Last Changed Rev'
   Last Changed Rev: 339202
   root@superman:/usr/ports #

   [2] 
http://lists.freebsd.org/pipermail/svn-ports-all/2014-January/041997.html


bye
Fabian
